target.name: SambaNova Systems
target.founder: Rodrigo Liang, Kunle Olukotun, Christopher Ré

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $5.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 11 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $23.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Tenstorrent
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.7B

data.narrative:
SambaNova Systems is a Series D-stage semiconductor design company founded by Rodrigo Liang, Kunle Olukotun, and Christopher Ré. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 11 same-sector comparables at similar valuation tiers, supplemented by three public semiconductor benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier to the company's $5.0B last post-money valuation, implying $7.5B. Peer-median methodology yields $2.3B, derived from the median across 11 private and public Chips & Semiconductors comparables, with a range spanning $1.0B to $23.0B. The dispersion reflects the heterogeneous valuations of peers including Cerebras ($23.0B, pre-IPO), PsiQuantum ($7.0B, private), and Tenstorrent ($2.7B, Series D). Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to the absence of disclosed revenue figures, though the sector median P/S multiple across public comps is 22.3x.

The two applicable methods support a triangulated range of $2.0B to $8.6B, with a median of $4.9B. The current $5.0B valuation sits near this median but above the peer-median anchor, reflecting potential stage-specific pricing premium. Key uncertainties include revenue scale, capital efficiency relative to peers, and whether the 1.5x step-up multiplier fully captures market conditions for Series D chip companies. The wide peer range signals sector volatility and the challenge of pricing pre-revenue or early-revenue frontier-tech hardware.
